region: Island of Capri, Bay of Naples
desc: Capri is best treated as a polished Bay of Naples island splurge rather than a lazy beach resort: dramatic boat arrivals, polished lanes around the Piazzetta, and a calmer Anacapri counterpoint all packed into a very small island. It works best for 1-3 nights or a tightly planned day trip, with the main trade-off being ferry logistics and crowd pressure once peak season hits.
hls: Blue Grotto when sea conditions allow
Gardens of Augustus views over the Faraglioni
Boat circuit past Capri's sea grottoes
Marina Grande to Capri-town funicular arrival
Villa San Michele and quieter Anacapri lanes

todo.name: Blue Grotto timing run
todo.desc: Go early and stay flexible, because sea state can close the grotto even on bright days.

todo.name: Monte Solaro chairlift from Anacapri
todo.desc: Ride up from Piazza Vittoria for the island's biggest panorama over Capri, the bay and the Sorrentine coast.

todo.name: Gardens of Augustus and Capri town walk
todo.desc: Use the terraced gardens for classic Faraglioni views, then loop back through the polished center before day-trippers thin out.

todo.name: Boat around the island
todo.desc: See the Faraglioni, White Grotto, Green Grotto and cliff-backed shoreline that you miss if you stay only on land.

todo.name: Villa San Michele
todo.desc: Base one Anacapri stop around Axel Munthe's villa for gardens, sea views and a break from Capri town crowds.

todo.name: Sunset aperitivo above Marina Piccola
todo.desc: End the day with a west-facing drink or short walk once excursion traffic drops and the island feels more balanced.

wifi.note: Usually strong in established hotels and upscale rentals, but ferry days, cliffside terraces, and old-stone buildings can make it feel less dependable than a mainland city break.

prac.fltNote: Capri has no airport; you normally fly to Naples, then transfer to Molo Beverello or Calata di Massa and continue by ferry or hydrofoil. Gatwick-Naples is the cleanest one-ticket pattern when schedules line up, while Rome works but usually turns the trip into a much longer rail-plus-port transfer. July-August ferry capacity and rough-sea disruption are usually the real pinch points, not the flight itself.
prac.lang: Italian; English is widely used around hotels, ferries, and restaurants
prac.visa: Italy applies Schengen entry rules; UK and Irish travellers should check passport validity and 90-in-180-day limits before travel.
alerts: Blue Grotto access can stop at short notice when swell or wind changes.
Hydrofoils are the first services to be disrupted in rough seas, so ferry backup matters in shoulder season.
July and August buses, funicular queues, and port transfers can eat more time than the island's size suggests.

sweet: Late April to June and September to early October are usually Capri's best balance of bright weather, running ferries, and manageable crowd levels; July and August are spectacular but much more logistically heavy.
